source_basis: German public solar and heat-pump review patterns
consumer guidance on PV and heat-pump offer evaluation
prototype sales-process requirements for post-quote follow-up

signals.name: ROI and cost-control planner
signals.definition: The buyer is evaluating the quote as an investment and needs assumptions, payback logic, and comparison context.
behavior_clues: Uploaded electricity bill or tariff details
Asks for payback period or total cost
Engages with finance or ROI sections of a proposal
Delays after seeing final quote value
signals.what_it_usually_means: The buyer is not necessarily cold; they may need a transparent assumptions review before signing.
signals.confidence_notes: High when explicit ROI or payback language is present. Medium when inferred only from price sensitivity.

task_fit.Phone Call: High when assumptions are complex or competitor comparison is active.
task_fit.Send Email: High for assumptions table, comparison recap, and written decision summary.
task_fit.Meeting in person: Medium only if ROI depends on site-specific facts or roof inspection.
task_fit.Send WhatsApp Video Message: Medium for short personalized walkthrough if consent exists.
task_fit.Send Gift: Low; does not resolve an analytical blocker.

mistakes_to_avoid: Do not claim guaranteed payback.
Do not hide assumptions behind simplified savings copy.
Do not discount before clarifying scope and value.

signals.name: Price shock
signals.definition: The buyer is reacting to the absolute quote amount or a perceived price gap.
behavior_clues: Asks for discount without discussing scope
Skips technical questions and focuses only on total amount
signals.what_it_usually_means: The buyer may need the quote decomposed into scope, alternatives, must-have versus optional items, and what is excluded.
signals.confidence_notes: High with explicit price language. Medium with quote-age silence after a high total.

task_fit.Phone Call: High if the buyer is actively comparing or confused.
task_fit.Send Email: High for itemized recap and option comparison.
task_fit.Meeting in person: Low unless price concern is tied to physical uncertainty.
task_fit.Send WhatsApp Video Message: Medium for a short scope walkthrough if consent exists.
task_fit.Send Gift: Very low; may look tone-deaf or manipulative.

mistakes_to_avoid: Do not treat all price shock as a liquidity problem.
Do not defend the price before clarifying scope.
Do not badmouth competitors.

signals.name: Comparison shopper
signals.definition: The buyer is comparing multiple providers, scopes, or recommendations before deciding.
signals.what_it_usually_means: The buyer needs help comparing scope, assumptions, exclusions, service, and risk, not a generic follow-up.
signals.confidence_notes: High when another offer or referral is explicit. Medium when the buyer asks broad fairness questions.

task_fit.Send Email: High for comparison matrix and scope recap.
task_fit.Meeting in person: Medium if comparison depends on roof/building inspection.
task_fit.Send Gift: Low; may distract from the comparison problem.

mistakes_to_avoid: Do not attack the competitor.
Do not assume the cheapest quote is equivalent.
Do not ignore excluded items, admin tasks, warranty, or installation quality.

signals.name: Risk and trust skeptic
signals.definition: The buyer is deciding whether the installer is reliable, respectful, and safe to trust with a high-ticket home project.
behavior_clues: Asks about warranty, insurance, or aftercare
Avoids committing until process is clear
signals.what_it_usually_means: The blocker is credibility and risk reduction; the next action should feel human, specific, and low pressure.
signals.confidence_notes: High when trust, pressure, damage, support, or warranty concerns are explicit.

task_fit.Phone Call: High if consent exists and a named human can answer concerns.
task_fit.Send Email: High for responsibility map, checklist, and written proof.
task_fit.Meeting in person: High if physical risk or house-specific trust concern exists.
task_fit.Send WhatsApp Video Message: Medium if personal explanation would lower friction.
task_fit.Send Gift: Low before trust is repaired; can feel like pressure.

mistakes_to_avoid: Do not use artificial urgency.
Do not say there is no risk.
Do not add another generic campaign before answering the concern.

signals.name: Technical-control optimizer
signals.definition: The buyer needs to understand system design, component choices, yield assumptions, or building fit before signing.
behavior_clues: Questions battery size or heat-pump fit
Has future EV or heat-pump load
signals.what_it_usually_means: The buyer needs a technical proof asset or expert explanation, not emotional reassurance.
signals.confidence_notes: High with explicit design, winter, battery, or heat-pump technical language.

task_fit.Send Email: High for chart, assumptions, and diagrams.
task_fit.Meeting in person: High if physical inspection is needed.
task_fit.Send WhatsApp Video Message: High for visual walkthrough of roof, chart, or quote change.

mistakes_to_avoid: Do not use blanket reassurance like panels work in winter without numbers.
Do not make claims when roof, load, or tariff inputs are missing.
Do not oversimplify technical tradeoffs.

signals.name: Independence and autarky optimizer
signals.definition: The buyer is motivated by using their own power, reducing supplier dependence, and planning future energy loads.
behavior_clues: Mentions EV or future heat pump
signals.what_it_usually_means: The buyer values autonomy but still needs realistic limits, especially seasonal limits and battery economics.
signals.confidence_notes: High with explicit independence/autarky language; medium when inferred from battery/EV interest.

task_fit.Meeting in person: Medium if future loads require site inspection.
task_fit.Send WhatsApp Video Message: High for visual roadmap or battery scenario explanation.

mistakes_to_avoid: Do not claim 100% independence.
Do not imply winter self-sufficiency without monthly data.
Do not frame battery only as ROI if autonomy is explicit.

signals.name: Climate impact confirmer
signals.definition: The buyer explicitly values emissions reduction, sustainability, renewable heating, or green power.
behavior_clues: Pairs PV with heat pump or green energy goals
Wants a shareable summary for another stakeholder
signals.what_it_usually_means: Impact framing can help, but should usually be paired with economics unless climate is the only explicit blocker.
signals.confidence_notes: High only when climate or CO2 language is explicit. Do not infer from demographics.

task_fit.Phone Call: Medium when another stakeholder needs numbers.
task_fit.Send Email: High for shareable climate plus assumptions summary.
task_fit.Meeting in person: Low unless other technical blockers exist.
task_fit.Send Gift: Medium only if it is useful and low-value, not a signing inducement.

mistakes_to_avoid: Do not rely on impact language if the partner is asking for payback.
Do not invent CO2 numbers without assumptions.
Do not infer environmental values from location or demographics.

signals.name: Stakeholder or advisor review
signals.definition: Another person must review or approve the decision before signature.
behavior_clues: Proposal forwarded or shared if tracking is permitted
Customer asks for a PDF summary
Customer delays after saying they need to discuss it
Another person joins calls or asks questions
signals.what_it_usually_means: The primary buyer may not be stuck; the decision group needs a shareable explanation.
signals.confidence_notes: High only when another stakeholder is explicitly mentioned.

task_fit.Phone Call: High for joint review if consent and availability exist.
task_fit.Meeting in person: Medium if household alignment requires physical walkthrough.

mistakes_to_avoid: Do not infer family status without explicit evidence.
Do not push the first contact to decide alone.
Do not send overly technical detail if the stakeholder needs an executive summary.

signals.name: Convenience and process-relief seeker
signals.definition: The buyer values someone handling paperwork, registration, coordination, and handoffs.
behavior_clues: Asks who handles grid or meter registration
Asks for clear next steps and responsibilities
signals.what_it_usually_means: The buyer may be persuaded by clarity and reduced effort, but vague all-in promises are risky.

task_fit.Send Email: High for responsibility map and checklist.
task_fit.Meeting in person: Medium for high-value trust or physical process questions.
task_fit.Send Gift: Medium after a meeting if useful and clearly non-conditional.

mistakes_to_avoid: Do not use vague carefree claims.
Do not promise external timeline control.
Do not hide customer-required steps.

signals.name: Site-specific decision blocker
signals.definition: The buyer needs someone to inspect or validate the home before they can trust the quote.
language_clues: jemand soll sich das Haus anschauen
behavior_clues: Raises roof condition, shade, building fit, or heat-load uncertainty
Does not accept virtual explanation as sufficient
Has old roof or complex building notes
signals.what_it_usually_means: A remote message will not resolve the blocker; the buyer needs physical validation or inspection.
signals.confidence_notes: High when customer explicitly requests a visit or physical concern is active.

task_fit.Phone Call: Medium as a bridge to schedule and define visit agenda.
task_fit.Send Email: Medium for confirming agenda and prep.
task_fit.Send WhatsApp Video Message: Low to medium only for pre-visit context.

mistakes_to_avoid: Do not keep sending generic emails if the customer wants physical validation.
Do not schedule a visit without a clear purpose.
Do not use travel-heavy visits for low-readiness stale leads.

signals.name: Ready-to-close hypothesis
signals.definition: The buyer has resolved major blockers and needs a clear final step to sign.
behavior_clues: Asked for contract or installation slot
No unresolved objection in latest debrief
signals.what_it_usually_means: The customer may need a concise recap and signature path, not more persuasion.
signals.confidence_notes: Treat as a hypothesis until signed; avoid overconfident labels.

task_fit.Phone Call: Medium for final confirmation if preferred.
task_fit.Send Email: High for signature recap and next steps.
task_fit.Meeting in person: Low unless final review is requested.
task_fit.Send WhatsApp Video Message: Medium as a personal recap if consent exists.
task_fit.Send Gift: Low before signing; avoid anything that looks like inducement.

mistakes_to_avoid: Do not add complexity when the buyer asks for next steps.
Do not ignore any unresolved stakeholder approval.
Do not label as certain until contract is signed.

signals.name: Stale or ghosting risk
signals.definition: The quote is aging without a clear blocker or next commitment.
behavior_clues: No reply after a promised follow-up
No valid tracking consent but no visible customer response
signals.what_it_usually_means: The lead may still be evaluating, but repeated generic follow-up can create pressure and reduce trust.
signals.confidence_notes: Medium unless customer explicitly disengaged. Lack of tracking consent means do not infer from opens/views.

task_fit.Phone Call: Low unless requested or consented and high-value.
task_fit.Send Email: Medium for one value-add question or recap.
task_fit.Send WhatsApp Video Message: Low unless opted in and a specific value-add exists.
task_fit.Send Gift: Very low; can look like pressure.

mistakes_to_avoid: Do not use tracking-derived assumptions without consent.
Do not send repeated generic follow-ups.
Do not manufacture urgency to revive a stale lead.
